title: Stroke
content_markdown:
#### Eligibility for Thrombolysis

- Age ≥ 18 years.
- Normal (or corrected) BGL (≥ 4mmol/L).
- Definite known time of onset < 24 hours from time of assessment (if not seen or patient woke with symptoms, time of onset was when they were last seen asymptomatic).
- Not requiring high-level nursing home care.
- Not terminally ill, nor permanently bed-ridden.
- **CRESST Stroke Screening Tool** score ≥ 4.

#### Management

- Assess using **Eligibility for Thrombolysis**. 
- Manage airway as per CMG 3 Airway Management.
- Do not hyperventilate – aim for EtCO2 27-40mmHg. 

- Posture for patient comfort.
- Check BGL 
- Treat hypoglycaemia cautiously – avoid hyperglycaemia.
- Treat hypotension as per CMG 14 Shock and Hypoperfusion.
- Aim for systolic BP >100mmHg.
- Complete **CRESST Stroke Screening Tool** early, and advise relevant stroke team if eligible. - 12‐lead ECG (do not delay transport for this).
- Minimise scene time.
- Notify hospital early (including thrombolysis eligibility), and promptly transport to the nearest hospital.

#### If Eligible for Thrombolysis or Clot Retrieval Pre-Notification

- Absolute minimum scene time consistent with complete assessment, including stroke screen and discussion with stroke team.
- Avoid analgesia or sedation in patients eligible for thrombolysis unless strongly indicated. 
- ECG monitoring is mandatory; report any occurrence of AF on hospital notification even if transient. - Wherever possible, transport a friend or relative who can verify onset time (at a minimum, record and handover a mobile phone number).
- If confident of success on first attempt, place 18g IVC in the ACF of the unaffected arm 
- If not confident of success with 1 attempt, withhold cannulation for patients eligible for thrombolysis.
- Radio notification to ED en route with ETA.
- On arrival at hospital: 

- Calvary: patient to remain on stretcher for imaging and decision regarding clot retrieval. Contact Comms and DO if no decision after 20 minutes 

- TCH: normal offload process 

#### NCH to TCH transfer for Clot Retrieval

- Patients at NCH who are deemed eligible for clot retrieval must be transferred to TCH urgently.
- If a patient is placed on an Alteplase infusion, please refer to the below guidance on how to manage the infusion during transport to TCH

#### Before leaving NCH

- Check with nursing staff that the supplied driver has adequate battery charge.
- It’s the NCH nurses responsibility to prepare the infusion, connect the driver to the patient and begin the infusion.
#### During transfer

- **Driver Management:**
When operating normally, the driver will continue to operate without any warning sound. If the driver emits a warning sound: 
- Check for occlusion – driver will display ‘line occluded’ 

- Ensure line is not kinked.

- Ensure patient’s arm is straight.

- Press Silent button (bell icon, No. 17)

- Restart the infusion by pressing Start button (green circle icon, No. 12)

- Check if infusion has completed – driver will display ‘end of infusion’ 

- Press Stop button (red stop, No. 13)

- Disconnect the driver’s line from the patient.

- Flush the cannula with 10mL NaCl.

- Turn off driver by holding On/Off button (No. 5)

- **Side Effects:**
Alteplase side effects are rare but can include allergic reactions and bleeding (including intracerebral haemorrhage). Signs of side effects include: 
- Rash
- Angioedema- Nausea and vomiting- New onset severe headache- Focal neurological changes- Sudden deterioration of response or ABCs
 If side effects are suspected: 
- Manage significant side effects within ACTAS scope and CMGs.
- Pause the infusion by pressing the Stop button (red stop, No. 13)- Call NCH Neurology (0427 144 111) for advice.
- If directed by NCH Neurology, resume infusion by pressing Start (green circle icon, No. 12).
- In NCH Neurology don’t answer, do not restart the infusion and continue to TCH. Ensure TCH are informed of infusion incompletion and nature of side effects.
#### After transfer

- Return the driver to NCH ED at earliest convenience.
- If driver return is not able to be facilitated prior to end of shift, contact the DO to inform them and to formulate a plan on how to deliver the driver back to NCH.
